---
title: "tbox-finder — Phase 0 (Foundation) dev-log"
author: "bioedca"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Phase 0 — Foundation

Per-step development log (CLAUDE.md §2.4). One stanza is appended after every
step's commit; this file renders to `docs/dev-log/phase0_<YYYY-MM-DD>.pdf` at
the phase-exit gate.

## Step P0-01 — 2026-07-07: Scaffold the repository to the PRD §16 layout

**Inputs.** PRD §16 layout spec.
**Process.** Created the §16 directory tree (`src/tbox_finder/`,
`workflow/{rules,profiles/slurm}`, `conf/{model,data,optim}`, `envs/`,
`slurm/`, `data/{raw,external,interim,processed}/`,
`tests/{unit,golden,ml,fixtures}/`, `analyses/`, `figures/`, `paper/`, `app/`,
`docker/`, `docs/decisions/`); authored `pyproject.toml` (setuptools src-layout
+ ruff/black/pytest config), a glob-including `workflow/Snakefile` with an empty
default `rule all`, `src/tbox_finder/__init__.py`, a `README.md` stub, and
`.gitkeep` markers in every empty leaf dir. `data/{raw,external}` are immutable
(`.gitkeep` only).
**Outputs.** Package skeleton importable as `tbox_finder`;
`tests/unit/test_import.py`, `tests/unit/test_layout.py`.
**Validation.** `PYTHONPATH=src python -c "import tbox_finder"` ✓; `pytest
tests/unit` green (2 files); layout-presence test ✓. `snakemake -n` **deferred**
to P0-05 (pinned env) / CI P0-08 — snakemake is not installable under the §3.1
env-flow before the first env exists (P0-05).
**Decisions.** Ran **in place**: P0-01 is pre-git-init — P0-02 performs `git
init` + the first commit — so no worktree/commit/CodeRabbit at this step
(drive-one-step: pre-git-init steps run in place; the P0-01 scaffold lands in
P0-02's first commit). No ADR.
**Commit.** deferred to P0-02 (first commit).

## Step P0-02 — 2026-07-07: Initialize git, publish the public GitHub repo, wire ignore/attributes/CodeRabbit

**Inputs.** P0-01 scaffold tree (working dir, no prior `.git`).
**Process.** `git init -b main` + repo-local `git lfs install`; authored
`.gitignore` (local-only docs `PRD/CLAUDE/imp/TODO/dev-manual.md`, `.dvc/config`,
`data/raw|external/**` with `.gitkeep` kept via negation, `**/wandb/`, secrets,
and a data-blob guard that deliberately spares `*.parquet`/`*.fa`/`*.fasta`/`*.cm`
since those are DVC- or git-LFS-managed), `.gitattributes` (LF normalization for
golden-hash reproducibility + git-LFS on `figures/**`, `*.cm`, `*.fa`, `*.fasta`,
`data/processed/splits/split_assignments.parquet`; `**/.gitkeep` kept plain),
and `.coderabbit.yaml` (review scoped to code-that-runs; `!`-glob `path_filters`
exclude `*.md`/`*.qmd`/ADRs/data/figures). The bootstrap first commit lands on
`main` — the "empty repo can't PR into itself" exception (CLAUDE.md §5.0); all
later code goes via worktree+PR. context7 (plugin server; the claude.ai server
was monthly-quota-exhausted) confirmed the `.coderabbit.yaml` v2 schema
(`reviews.path_filters`, `!`-prefixed excludes); git-lfs 3.5.1 installed to
`~/.local/bin` as a VCS dev tool (outside the `envs/*` lock flow, §3.1).
**Outputs.** `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes`, `.coderabbit.yaml`; git repo on
`main`; `origin` = `https://github.com/bioedca/tbox-finder.git` (**public**).
**Validation.** Local gates ✓ — `git check-ignore` matches all 5 local-only
docs; `git lfs track` lists 5 LFS patterns (+ `**/.gitkeep` excluded); zero
binaries staged (largest blob 2.4 KB). Push gates ✓ — `gh api user -q .login`
= **bioedca**; repo visibility = **public** (verified this session). CodeRabbit
zero-finding prose-diff gate: **pending the first PR** (Stop, CLAUDE.md §7).
**Decisions.** No `LICENSE` file authored — README asserts code=MIT but the
copyright holder's legal name is unresolved; deferred rather than fabricate a
legal holder (§10.3 no-fabrication spirit). No ADR.
**Commit.** (this commit — the repository's first).

## Step P0-03 — 2026-07-08: Author ADR-0001 — Architecture & aims (the contract ADR)

**Inputs.** PRD §1, §2 (esp. §2.3), §5, §6, §18.3.
**Process.** Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0001-architecture-and-aims.md` — the
first ADR and the *contract* ADR, written first so every later seed ADR + gate
step inherits the locked precedence + terminal-outcome map. It locks D1 the
two-stage Caduceus-PS→RiNALMo architecture (8-class identifiers, sequence-only
Stage-2, two Stage-1 checkpoints); D2 primary/secondary aims + G-A…G-E; D3
claim & gate precedence (GATE-1 central + precondition for GATE-3; GATE-3 split
method-sub-gate/breadth-floor; calibrated-negative PASS); D4 the precedence
carve-out reproduced **verbatim** from §2.3; D5 GATE-1-failure parity-segmenter
+ P4→P5 go/no-go; D6 the **three distinct failure branches** (B1 outright→parity;
B2 underpowered→§7 halt, re-power/resource-paper; B3 GATE-4/GATE-2 method-gate→
ADR-0002 NT-multispecies/continued-pretraining backbone fallbacks first); D7 the
project-level rollup + discovery-predominantly-inconclusive terminal outcome
(rollup function pinned in ADR-0006); D8 intended-use posture; D9 release-license
gating (code MIT + weights CC-BY-4.0 pinned per PRD header; dataset license
delegated to the P0 audit); D10 delegations to ADR-0002…0006 (no values pinned
here). Pins **no threshold values** (all delegated). Adversarial faithfulness
sweep (5 parallel verifiers over the PRD sections) caught one fabrication — an
early D9 draft wrongly framed the *code* license as unresolved; corrected to the
PRD-pinned **code = MIT**, reserving the P0-audit delegation for the *dataset*
license only.
**Outputs.** `docs/decisions/ADR-0001-architecture-and-aims.md` (+ its embedded
cross-reference impact list).
**Validation.** All three failure branches stated distinctly ✓; precedence
carve-out verbatim vs PRD §2.3 ✓; adversarial faithfulness sweep clean after the
license fix ✓; **user sign-off recorded 2026-07-08** ✓ (CLAUDE.md §7 item 2).
CodeRabbit zero-finding prose-diff gate: on this PR (prose-only ADR → expected
zero findings, correct scoping not a missing review).
**Decisions.** ADR-0001 pins no numbers by design — delegation + the §2.3
precedence carve-out keep every threshold authoritative in its owning ADR and
blinded-frozen at P0. Cross-ref ADR-0001.
**Commit.** (this commit — the P0-03 atomic commit on branch `p0-03-adr-0001`).

## Step P0-04 — 2026-07-08: Author ADR-0002 — Environment & ML stack

**Inputs.** PRD §3.1 (CLAUDE.md), §10.1–10.3, §15, §18.3; ADR-0001 (D5 swapped-
backbone integrity, D6 branch-B3 backbone fallbacks). Every version fact verified
against authoritative sources on **2026-07-08** (assistant cutoff is Jan 2026;
context7 §3.3) — a 6-agent research fan-out over PyPI JSON, `download.pytorch.org`,
GitHub release asset lists, the Caduceus HF config/modeling files, and the RiNALMo
paper.
**Process.** Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0002-environment-and-ml-stack.md`
pinning D1 the per-env conda-lock policy (five envs, one lock each, **Python 3.12**
forced by numpy 2.5.1/scipy 1.18; the deprecated `pytorch-cuda` metapackage
replaced by a pinned `pip:` cu128 block — a mechanism update within PRD §15 intent,
flagged per precedence); D2 the load-bearing stack with a named version + rationale
per pin, gated by two ecosystem ceilings — **`transformers` 4.57.5** (Caduceus
`trust_remote_code` unvalidated on v5) and the sm_86-kernel torch ceiling; D3 the
**`torch` 2.7.1+cu128 anchor** (Option B — `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3 source-built to
avoid the Mamba-3 Hopper-dep liability; `causal-conv1d` 1.6.2.post1 + `flash-attn`
2.8.3.post1 prebuilt), Option A all-prebuilt (torch 2.5) as the pre-registered
P0-06 fallback, Option C (Mamba-3) rejected, + the confirmed pure-PyTorch
selective-scan / SDPA fallbacks; D4 the CI Docker image; D5 the RiNALMo parity
numerics — **N = ±2 pp** (paper reports no per-split SD) with a telomerase carve-out
and a magnitude rationale, + the `multimolecule` 0.0.9 mirror pin + the env-split
contingency; D6 the RNA-FM fallback phasing (a–d) + the swapped backbone re-clearing
the **absolute GATE-1/GATE-2** gates and re-running the P4→P5 go/no-go; D7 the
Caduceus two-part transfer go/no-go + continued-pretraining/NT-multispecies ladder
(ADR-0001 B3). D8 never-bump discipline; D9 delegations.
**Outputs.** `docs/decisions/ADR-0002-environment-and-ml-stack.md` (+ its embedded
cross-reference impact list). No DVC artifact (prose ADR).
**Validation.** Every load-bearing pin carries a named version + rationale ✓;
RiNALMo parity margin **N = 2 pp** numeric ✓; the two-part Caduceus go/no-go + the
RNA-FM-swap re-clearing conditions written per §10.2/§10.3/§2.3 ✓; **user sign-off
recorded 2026-07-08** ✓ (CLAUDE.md §7 item 2, Option B as-is). CodeRabbit
zero-finding prose-diff gate on this PR (prose-only ADR + `.qmd` → expected zero
findings, correct scoping not a missing review).
**Decisions.** Anchor chosen for *correctness + reproducibility over recency*: torch
is set by the kernel ecosystem, not by torch itself; the newest `mamba-ssm` 2.3.x
(Mamba-3) was rejected because its hard `quack-kernels`/`tilelang`/`apache-tvm-ffi`
deps are Hopper/Blackwell-oriented and of unverified Ampere-import-safety — a
load-bearing liability for zero Caduceus (Mamba-2) benefit. N=±2 pp grounded in the
RiNALMo paper's 0.01 reporting granularity + same-weights seed/reimpl noise, with the
0.12-F1 telomerase outlier carved out. Cross-ref ADR-0002; parity cite `PMID:40593636
/ DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60872-5 / PMC12219582` (accessed 2026-07-08).
**Commit.** (this commit — the P0-04 atomic commit on branch `p0-04-adr-0002`).

## Step P0-05 — 2026-07-08: Build the five conda-lock environments (per-env lockfiles)
**Inputs.** ADR-0002 D1 (per-env conda-lock policy) + D2/D3 (the pinned stack).
`conda-lock` 4.0.2 (installed as an isolated dev env — git-lfs→`~/.local/bin`
precedent; not a member of any `envs/*.yml`).
**Process.** Authored the five `envs/*.yml` specs (`conda-lock lock -p linux-64`
per-env, never aggregate; channels conda-forge[/bioconda][/nvidia]). Every pin
traces to ADR-0002 D2; the `viz`/`app` toolchains (ADR-delegated pins) were selected
and conda-forge-version-verified at build time. Locked + installed + import-smoked
the four CPU envs. The **`ml` lock is deferred to P0-06** (user decision 2026-07-08):
under the signed-off Option-B anchor `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3 is source-only (no
torch-2.7 wheel; sdist carries no static metadata) so it needs `nvcc` + `sm_86` —
absent on the laptop (`sm_89` GPU, no nvcc); P0-06 is its ADR-native home (the sm_86
wheel/build-availability check). `envs/ml.yml` is authored in full as the spec P0-06
locks. Added `tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py` (stdlib-only) and excluded the
machine-generated `*.conda-lock.yml` from CodeRabbit.
**Outputs.** `envs/{data,infernal,viz,app}.yml` + their `*.conda-lock.yml` (real
solves — `data` 305 pkgs); `envs/ml.yml` (lock → P0-06);
`tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py`; ADR-0002 **Amendment A1**; `.coderabbit.yaml`
(`!**/*.conda-lock.yml`). No DVC artifact (env specs + locks are git-tracked).
**Validation.** `conda-lock install` reproduced all four CPU envs + import-smoke
PASS — infernal (INFERNAL 1.1.5, ViennaRNA 2.7.2, Bio 1.87, numpy 2.5.1); data
(snakemake 9.23.1, dvc 3.67.1, polars 1.42.1, pandas 2.3.3, scikit-learn 1.9.0,
numpy 2.5.1); viz (matplotlib 3.11.0, seaborn 0.13.2, quarto 1.9.38); app (node
26.4.0 / npm 11.17.0, confirmed by absolute-path check — the login-shell smoke's
v22.22.2 was an nvm-PATH artifact, not the env). `pytest tests/unit` 7/7 ✓; ruff
check + format ✓. The `ml` kernel import/forward smoke is deferred to P1 (cluster
GPU, ADR-0002 D3). Pins match ADR-0002 (data pandas 2.3.3 per Amendment A1).
**Decisions.** Two stop-and-asks resolved with user sign-off. (1) **`ml` lock
deferred to P0-06** rather than front-running P0-07's Docker image or a §9.3 cluster
job. (2) **`data` pandas 3.0.3 → 2.3.3** — bioconda `snakemake-executor-plugin-slurm`
2.7.1 over-caps `pandas<3.0` (contradicting the same version's upstream PyPI `<4`);
the pure-conda downgrade to the max 2.x keeps the load-bearing numpy-2.5.1 ABI and
forgoes only non-load-bearing pandas-3.0 features (polars 1.42.1 is the dataframe
workhorse). Recorded as ADR-0002 **Amendment A1** + a D2 pin-coupling bump-trigger;
`viz` pandas aligned to 2.3.3 for repo consistency. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D1/D2/D3 + A1.
**Commit.** (this commit — the P0-05 atomic commit on branch `p0-05-envs`).

## Step P0-06 — 2026-07-08: sm_86 wheel-availability check + pinned pure-PyTorch fallbacks
**Inputs.** `envs/ml.yml` (the Option-B kernel pins: torch 2.7.1+cu128, `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3,
`causal-conv1d` 1.6.2.post1, `flash-attn` 2.8.3.post1, cp312/sm_86); ADR-0002 §D3 (the wheel +
fallback claims to empirically confirm).
**Process.** Authored `scripts/check_sm86_wheels.py`, a stdlib PyPI-JSON + GitHub-Releases-REST
metadata probe (no GPU) that classifies each kernel `wheel-present`/`source-build-required`/
`unavailable` at the two D3 torch anchors, and ran it (token-forced GitHub). Independently
confirmed each torch-2.7 wheel is a real downloadable asset via a Range-GET probe, ran a 3-agent
adversarial verification, and read the raw `mamba-ssm` v2.2.6.post3 source to settle the fallback
question; context7 confirmed the `mamba` `use_fast_path` knob + the `multimolecule` RiNALMo
HF-attention path. Amended ADR-0002 (A2) and re-pinned `envs/ml.yml` to the three release-asset
wheel URLs.
**Outputs.** `scripts/check_sm86_wheels.py`; `tests/unit/test_check_sm86_wheels.py` (10 cases);
ADR-0002 **Amendment A2**; `envs/ml.yml` (three kernels pinned by exact release-asset URL, nvcc
re-scoped to DeepSpeed-JIT, lockfile note reversed → laptop-lockable). No DVC artifact.
**Validation.** Gate PASSED — all three kernels **wheel-present at Option B** (torch2.7/cu12/cp312/
linux, abiTRUE): `flash-attn` (256 MB), `causal-conv1d` (194 MB), `mamba-ssm` (533 MB, 8,877
downloads). `pytest tests/unit` 16/16; ruff + black clean. The GPU import/forward smoke stays P1.
**Decisions.** The probe **disproved** D3's premise that `mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3 must be
source-built (**C1**: a prebuilt cu12torch2.7 wheel exists → Option B needs zero source builds,
`ml.yml` pinned by URL, P0-05 ml-lock deferral reversed to laptop-lockable) and **disproved**
D3's pure-PyTorch selective-scan fallback (**C2**: the packaged `Mamba` bare-imports
`selective_scan_cuda`; `use_fast_path=False` calls the CUDA kernel, not `selective_scan_ref`, so
a missing kernel is a capability loss — only `causal_conv1d`→`nn.Conv1d` is speed-only; P1 must
assert `import selective_scan_cuda`). RiNALMo SDPA/eager fallback confirmed (**K1**). User
sign-off recorded 2026-07-08. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D3 + **A2**. Verification: artifact-based (probe
+ download-confirm + 3-agent adversarial pass + raw-source read).
**Commit.** (this commit — the P0-06 atomic commit on branch `p0-06-sm86-wheels`).

## Step P0-06c — 2026-07-08: split the `ml` env into `ml-dna` + `ml-rna`, lock both
**Inputs.** The P0-06b `ml.yml` (the proven torch 2.7.1+cu128 16-URL closure + three sm_86
kernels); ADR-0002 D2/D3/D5 + A2; the two P0-06b findings carried over (conda-lock 4.0.2 cannot
lock the cu128 stack via an index; `multimolecule` 0.0.9 needs transformers 5.x).
**Process.** Confirmed via PyPI `Requires-Dist` + the conda-forge 5.13.0 recipe run-deps + the
transformers v5.0.0 release notes (context7 §3.3, plugin server; claude.ai server quota-blocked)
that transformers **5.13.0** (newest) caps `huggingface_hub<2.0,>=1.5.0` / `tokenizers<=0.23.0,>=0.22.0`
/ `safetensors>=0.8.0`, and that `multimolecule` 0.0.9 (uploaded 2026-02-02, after transformers
5.0.0) declares no transformers cap but imports `transformers.initialization` at runtime. Authored
`envs/ml-dna.yml` (= old `ml.yml` minus `multimolecule`, transformers 4.57.5) and `envs/ml-rna.yml`
(ml-dna + exactly three forced HF-core changes: transformers→5.13.0, hub→1.22.0, safetensors→0.8.0,
plus `multimolecule` 0.0.9; tokenizers stays 0.22.2 since 0.23.0 is absent on conda-forge and 0.23.1
exceeds the cap). Both carry the identical URL-pinned cu128 closure. Locked both (`conda-lock -p
linux-64`), `conda-lock install`-reproduced both on the laptop (no GPU/nvcc), CPU import-smoked both.
Deleted `envs/ml.yml`; wrote ADR-0002 **A4** (folding in the retired A3 draft); extended
`tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py`.
**Outputs.** `envs/ml-dna.yml` + `envs/ml-dna.conda-lock.yml`; `envs/ml-rna.yml` +
`envs/ml-rna.conda-lock.yml`; `envs/ml.yml` **deleted**; ADR-0002 **Amendment A4**;
`tests/unit/test_env_manifest.py` (EXPECTED_ENVS → data/infernal/**ml-dna/ml-rna**/viz/app; both ml
locks required; torch-URL + no-`--extra-index-url` guard over both ml envs). No DVC artifact
(env specs + locks git-tracked).
**Validation.** Gate PASSED. Both `conda-lock` solve (ml-dna 52 s, ml-rna 72 s) + `conda-lock install`
reproduced locally with no GPU/nvcc; lockfile audit clean for both — torch **2.7.1+cu128**, 14
`nvidia-*` + `triton` + the 3 kernels all at pinned URLs, **no `--extra-index-url` leak**. ml-dna
smoke: torch 2.7.1 CPU matmul + transformers **4.57.5** + `CaduceusConfig` via `trust_remote_code`.
ml-rna smoke: transformers **5.13.0** + `transformers.initialization` importable + **`import
multimolecule` + `RiNALMoModel` import/instantiate — the P0-06b blocker, now cleared** (attn=eager,
SDPA-capable). `pytest tests/unit` 23/23; ruff + black clean. GPU kernel forward smoke (incl.
`import selective_scan_cuda`) + RiNALMo parity stay P1.
**Decisions.** Realized D5's pre-registered env split; the "multimolecule 0.0.9 era-matched to
4.57.5" premise (D2/D5) is **disproven** (needs v5). Minimize-divergence: ml-rna differs from ml-dna
in only three HF-core pins + multimolecule (accelerate 1.12.0 / peft 0.18.1 / datasets 4.5.0 already
admit hub 1.x + transformers 5). Durable operational finding: both envs bundle `cuda-nvcc` 12.8 for
DeepSpeed JIT and DeepSpeed probes `CUDA_HOME` at import (on the RNA side via
multimolecule→danling→deepspeed), so **runtime must export `CUDA_HOME=$CONDA_PREFIX`** (recorded in
A4; Snakemake/sbatch will set it). A3 draft never committed → folded into A4; no standalone A3.
User sign-off recorded 2026-07-08. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D5 + **A4**. Verification: artifact-based
(conda-lock solve/install logs + lockfile pin audit + 16-URL live probe + two CPU smokes).
**Commit.** (this commit — the P0-06c atomic commit on branch `p0-06c-ml-split`).

## Step P0-07 — 2026-07-08: Build the CI Docker image (CUDA 12.8 + PyTorch, GHCR)
**Inputs.** `envs/ml-dna.conda-lock.yml` + `envs/ml-rna.conda-lock.yml` (P0-06c), ADR-0002 D4 (as
amended by A2 C1 + A4). **Process.** Authored `docker/Dockerfile` (`docker/Dockerfile`) on the
`nvidia/cuda:12.8.1-cudnn-devel-ubuntu24.04` base (digest `sha256:24c8e358…`; latest 12.8 patch,
tag existence probed on Docker Hub), installing Miniforge **25.3.1-0** → `conda-lock` **4.0.2** (the
exact lock-generation version) → **both** locked envs via `conda-lock install --name <env> --mamba
--copy` (`--copy` avoids cross-layer hardlinks; `--validate-platform` default asserts linux-64). A
whitelist `.dockerignore` sends ONLY the two lock files into the build context (227 kB; no `.git`,
data, or secrets). An inline entrypoint activates `${TBOX_ENV:-ml-dna}` and exports
`CUDA_HOME=$CONDA_PREFIX` (ADR-0002 operational note). Built + smoked locally (BuildKit).
**Outputs.** `docker/Dockerfile`, `.dockerignore`; local image `tbox-finder:p0-07`
(`sha256:2c547f8f…`, **34.6 GB**). No DVC artifact (Dockerfile git-tracked; the image is a
CI/GHCR build artifact). GHCR push is wired to the CI schedule/release trigger in **P0-08** (D4:
built on schedule/release, not per-push). **Validation.** Gate PASSED. `docker build` exit 0; four
import smokes green — two build-time (`conda run` per env) + two `docker run` end-to-end through the
entrypoint: **ml-dna** = torch **2.7.1+cu128** / `torch.version.cuda` **12.8** / transformers
**4.57.5**; **ml-rna** = same torch + transformers **5.13.0** + `import multimolecule` **0.0.9**
(the P0-06b blocker, exercised again in-container); the three prebuilt kernel wheels present in both
(`mamba-ssm` 2.2.6.post3, `causal-conv1d` 1.6.2.post1, `flash-attn` 2.8.3.post1, metadata-read — the
GPU kernel *forward* smoke incl. `import selective_scan_cuda` stays P1, ADR-0002 A2 C2); entrypoint
sets `CUDA_HOME` to the active env prefix. **Decisions.** `-devel` base retained ONLY for DeepSpeed
JIT ZeRO ops (A2 C1 killed the mamba source build → zero source builds; `cuda-nvcc` 12.8 ships in
both conda envs). Installs BOTH locks per the A4 env split (the retired single `ml` lock is gone).
Entrypoint uses `set -eo pipefail` **without `-u`**: conda's `cuda-nvcc` activation
(`~cuda-nvcc_activate.sh`) references unset `NVCC_PREPEND_FLAGS`, which nounset aborts on — a bug the
end-to-end `docker run` caught that the build-time `conda run` smoke masked. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D4 /
A2 C1 / A4. Verification: artifact-based (BuildKit build log + four container import smokes).
**Commit.** (this commit — the P0-07 atomic commit on branch `p0-07-docker`).

## Step P0-08 — 2026-07-08: GitHub Actions CI workflow (lint / DAG / tests / Docker)
**Inputs.** Repo scaffold (`pyproject.toml` tool configs, `workflow/Snakefile`, `src/tbox_finder`,
`tests/unit`), the committed `envs/data.conda-lock.yml` (+ ml-dna/ml-rna locks for the Docker job),
`docker/Dockerfile` (P0-07), ADR-0002 (env stack), the CLAUDE.md §8.6 CI contract. **Process.**
Authored `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with four jobs. `lint` and `test` run on GitHub-hosted
`ubuntu-latest` via `actions/setup-python` 3.12 with pinned dev tools (`ruff==0.15.15`,
`black==25.11.0`, `pytest==9.0.2`) — these live in no science env (the conda-lock/git-lfs
isolated-dev-tool precedent). `dag` reproduces the committed `data` env with
`conda-incubator/setup-miniconda` (Miniforge) + `conda-lock 4.0.2` (the exact lock-generation
version, mirroring `docker/Dockerfile`) and runs `snakemake --lint` + `snakemake -n` inside it
(`conda run -n data …`; snakemake 9.23.1, CLAUDE.md §3.2 rule=env). `docker` builds + pushes the ML
image to GHCR, `if`-gated to `schedule`/`release`/`workflow_dispatch` only (never per-PR).
Added scaffold placeholder tests so `tests/{golden,ml}` collect ≥1 test — with unique module
basenames, since pytest's prepend import-mode collides on duplicate names when the dirs lack
`__init__.py`. **Outputs.** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`; `tests/golden/test_golden_scaffold.py`;
`tests/ml/test_ml_scaffold.py`. No DVC artifact (all git-tracked). **Validation.** Gate = CI green
on the PR that adds it. Pre-verified locally with the exact CI versions: `ruff check` exit 0,
`black --check` exit 0 (black 25.11.0), `pytest tests/{unit,golden,ml}` **25 passed**,
`snakemake --lint`/`-n` exit 0 in `tbox-finder-data` (snakemake 9.23.1). **Decisions.** ruff/black/
pytest pinned as isolated dev tools (not added to any science env), matching local practice + the
conda-lock/git-lfs precedent; snakemake stays science-pipeline tooling, run inside its pinned
conda-lock env. The `docker` job is schedule/release-only per ADR-0002 D4 / CLAUDE.md §8.6; the
~34.6 GB image (P0-07) vs. a GitHub runner's disk is a flagged risk validated on the first
scheduled/dispatched build (a disk-reclaim step buys headroom), not on this per-PR gate. `push`
scoped to `branches-ignore: main` since `main` only receives squash-merges. Cross-ref ADR-0002 D4,
CLAUDE.md §8.6 / §3.2. **Commit.** (this commit — the P0-08 atomic commit on branch `p0-08-ci`).

## Step P0-09 — 2026-07-08: Author ADR-0003 — Cluster & scan ops

**Inputs.** PRD §7.2 (corpora, de-dup, sizing/feasibility lever, minimum-viable scan), §15
(cluster topology, DVC-capacity rule, compute ceiling + patience budget, training/sweep budget,
shard-failure + node-outage rules), §18.1–18.3 (P0 deliverable, risks, ADR-0003 delegation line);
CLAUDE.md §9 (cluster protocol); ADR-0001 (contract/precedence, format template).
**Process.** Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0003-cluster-and-scan-ops.md` (D1–D10) pinning the
cluster/SLURM contract (D1: `gpu`/a4000, no `--account`/QOS/`--nodelist`/`compute`/`zero`,
artifact-based verification since `sacct` off), Snakemake-executor-CPU-only / GPU-via-hand-authored-
`sbatch` (D2), the estimate-anchored DVC-capacity rule (D3, concrete path/capacity forwarded to
P0-10), corpus de-dup `GTDB-rep > RefSeq-complete > MAG` (D4), corpus priority + minimum-viable
scan = tiers (1)+(2) + breadth-preserving feasibility lever (D5), the compute-ceiling determination
rule + patience budget (D6, numeric **frozen at the pre-P5 sizing gate**), the training/sweep
GPU-hour budget rule (D7, numeric frozen at P1 first measurement), and the two P0-numeric ops
rules. Draft was adversarially checked pre-sign-off by a 4-lens subagent sweep (PRD-faithfulness /
completeness / internal-consistency / validation-gate); 8 minor/nit cross-reference + rationale
fixes applied (blinded-freeze re-cited to ADR-0005, scan-plan-execution sub-gate to ADR-0006,
ADR-0004 added to Related/impact list, D4↔D5 corpus tiers aligned, D8/D9 magnitude rationales
added). **Outputs.** `docs/decisions/ADR-0003-cluster-and-scan-ops.md`
(sha256 `0a0bff87…c975d599f`, 125 lines).
**Validation.** Gate = compute-ceiling **rule** pinned with numeric explicitly deferred to the
pre-P5 sizing gate (D6) ✓; de-dup priority (D4) + minimum-viable-scan spec (D5) written ✓;
shard-failure fraction **≤ 2%** (D8) + node-outage hold **72 h** (D9) numeric ✓; **user sign-off
2026-07-08** ✓ — **PASS**. **Decisions.** Two P0 numbers pinned as pre-registered *ops* thresholds
(D8 ≤ 2%; D9 72 h) — not scientific-claim gates, so no ADR-0001-D4/ADR-0005 blinded-freeze, but
change-by-ADR-sign-off only; each threshold's sole failure mode is a CLAUDE.md §7 stop-and-ask, so
both stay conservative. Compute ceiling + patience-budget days deferred to the sizing gate because
the binding number needs the P2 scanner's Stage-1 candidate density (PRD §7.2/§15). Snakemake SLURM
executor deliberately barred for GPU (auto-retry would bypass the §9.3 one-shot ack). Cross-ref
ADR-0001 (D3 non-circularity), ADR-0005 (recall floor), ADR-0006 (GATE-3 scan-plan-execution +
rollup); PRD §7.2/§15/§18. No DVC artifact (prose ADR, git-tracked). **Commit.** (this commit — the
P0-09 atomic commit on branch `p0-09-adr-0003`).

## Step P0-10 — 2026-07-08: DVC init + dvc-ssh cluster-scratch remote + capacity pin & verify

**Inputs.** ADR-0003 D3 (estimate-anchored DVC-capacity rule); cluster scratch via `ssh two`;
`tbox-finder-data` env (dvc 3.67.1 + dvc-ssh 4.3.0 + asyncssh 2.23.0); the tboxevo `provenance.json`
convention (CLAUDE.md §11). **Process.** `dvc init` (committed scaffold `.dvc/.gitignore` +
`.dvcignore`; `.dvc/config` and `.dvc/config.local` both stay gitignored per §5.2). Added the
`dvc-ssh` remote `cluster-scratch` → `ssh://ecc1695@two/onedata/mondragonlab/ecc1695/tbox-finder/
dvc-remote` to per-machine `.dvc/config.local`. Discovered the scratch mount by `ssh two df`:
`/onedata` (shared beegfs, user-owned dir) was chosen over the 88 %-full HOME (`/scr/exports`, 691 GB
free) and over `/twodata` (19 TB free) for its shared-FS reachability from the `gpu` nodes + most
headroom. Wrote `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py` (`write_provenance` / `build_provenance` /
`sha256_file` / `git_sha` / `env_lock_hash`) + `tests/unit/test_provenance.py` (13 tests), wiring the
`env_lock_hash` that tboxevo had left a `null` stub. **Outputs.** committed scaffold; committed
capacity record `docs/dev-log/P0-10-dvc-capacity.md`; `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py` +
`tests/unit/test_provenance.py`; per-machine `.dvc/config.local` (uncommitted).
**Validation.** ADR-0003 D3 gate = `ssh two df` free **32.9 TB ≥ 2 TB provisional P5 estimate**
(≈ 16× headroom) → **PASS**; `dvc remote list` shows the default remote; `dvc status` clean;
`provenance.json` carries all six §11 stamps (rule / script / git-SHA / env-lock-hash / seed /
input+output sha256) — asserted on real repo files; `pytest tests/unit` **36 passed** (23 + 13 new);
ruff + black clean; dvc-ssh **connectivity smoke** (throwaway `dvc push` round-trip through the
`zero`→`two` ProxyJump, object at `dvc-remote/files/md5/33/7bf06…`, then cleaned up) **PASS**.
**Decisions.** DVC-tracked artifacts exclude the multi-TB raw genome corpora (streamed to node-local
`/tmp`, discarded — ADR-0003 D1/D3), so the 2 TB provisional budget covers only checkpoints +
interim/processed tables + training/eval datasets; the estimate is an *ops* engineering number (no
ADR-0005 blinded-freeze / no §10.1 gate) and re-verifies when the pre-P5 sizing gate (ADR-0003 D6)
freezes the real volume. **Durable finding:** the encrypted `id_ed25519` breaks dvc-ssh if `keyfile`
is set (*"Passphrase must be specified…"*) → omit `keyfile`, rely on the ssh-agent (`allow_agent`
default), matching interactive `ssh two`. context7 §3.3: dvc-ssh remote-config + ssh-config
resolution order confirmed (claude.ai Context7 quota-blocked → plugin server). **Commit.** (this
commit — the P0-10 atomic commit on branch `p0-10-dvc-provenance`).

## Step P0-11 — 2026-07-08: W&B offline scaffold + login-node `wandb sync` rule + `huggingface_hub` scaffold
**Inputs.** The locked `ml-dna`/`ml-rna` envs (wandb 0.28.0, huggingface_hub 0.35.3 — ADR-0002); the
`conf/` Hydra groups; `workflow/Snakefile`'s auto-include of `rules/*.smk`; `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py`
as the bare-env-import style precedent. **Process.** Added `conf/tracking/wandb.yaml` (a Hydra
config-group option defaulting `mode: offline` — SLURM `gpu` nodes have no reliable outbound for live
streaming, CLAUDE.md §9.2/§11 — with `project`/`entity: null`/`dir: wandb`, no secret); the first repo
`.smk` rule module `workflow/rules/setup.smk::wandb_sync` (login-node `wandb sync --sync-all {offline_dir:q}`,
`conda: ../../envs/ml-dna.yml`, `log:`, kept out of `rule all`); and `src/tbox_finder/hub.py` — a release-time
(CLAUDE.md §6.3) HF push/pull helper (`resolve_token`/`push_folder`/`pull_snapshot`/`push_model`/`push_dataset`)
that **defers** every `huggingface_hub` import into its functions so the module loads in a bare env, and
authenticates **only** from `HF_TOKEN`/`HUGGINGFACE_HUB_TOKEN` (token never logged/persisted). **Outputs.**
`conf/tracking/wandb.yaml`; `workflow/rules/setup.smk`; `src/tbox_finder/hub.py`; `tests/unit/test_hub_scaffold.py`
(5 guards: bare-env import, env-only token precedence, offline default, sync-rule shape, no committed secret).
**Validation.** All PASS — `wandb sync --help` reachable from `tbox-ml-dna`; `mode: offline` is the compute-node
default; `test_no_committed_secrets` + secret-free files (no HF/W&B key literal); `import tbox_finder.hub`
succeeds in the base env with `huggingface_hub` **absent** (`pytest tests/unit` **41 passed**, 36 + 5 new);
`snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `snakemake -n` clean (first `.smk` rule lint-clean via `conda:` + `log:`);
`ruff` + `black` 25.11.0 clean. **Decisions.** Offline+login-node-sync (not the Snakemake SLURM executor) per
PRD §16 / CLAUDE.md §9.3 — the executor auto-retries, bypassing the one-shot submit-ack. `hub.py` mirrors the
`provenance.py` deferred-import discipline so it imports in the bare CI test env (huggingface_hub lives only in
the `ml-*` envs). HF push is release-time only (§6.3); this is scaffold only, unwired from the DAG/training loop.
context7 §3.3 (claude.ai Context7 quota-blocked → plugin server): wandb `sync --sync-all` + `WANDB_MODE=offline`;
huggingface_hub `HfApi.upload_folder`/`snapshot_download` + `HF_TOKEN` env auth (token not persisted). No DVC
artifact (all git-tracked). **Commit.** (this commit — the P0-11 atomic commit on branch `p0-11-wandb-hf-scaffold`).

## Step P0-11a — 2026-07-09: Stage the §7.1 reference assets into `data/external/refs/`
**Inputs.** Immutable sibling laptop checkouts (PRD §7.1 raw inputs, `sources_root=..`): `tboxdb-master/`
(class-I `RF00230.cm`; the four `master_datasets/MASTER/*_master.fa` — the lowercase `*_master.fa` glob, which
excludes the uppercase `gecont3_full_MASTER.fa`), `tbox-scan-master/` (class-II `TBDB001.cm`, NAME `seed_ILE`),
and `tboxevo/data/processed/cms/` (the structural sub-element CMs `idtm_seq_aware.cm` / `idtm_structure_only.cm`
/ `idtm_structure_only.amendment2.cm` / `stem2_structure_only.cm`, and the 18-record class-II
`heldout_blind_set.fasta`). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/refs.py::stage_refs` (stdlib-only; reuses
`provenance.sha256`/`git_sha`) copies each of the 11 assets, verifies it against a **pinned SHA-256** on both
source and copy (fail-loud, §10.3), and writes `data/external/refs/provenance.json` carrying per-asset
source-path/version/accessed-date(2026-07-09)/license/sha256/bytes. Driven by the first `workflow/rules/data.smk`
rule `stage_reference_assets` (LOCAL, kept out of `rule all`; no `input:` — sources are external; `dest_dir`
derived from `output.manifest` so `--lint` is clean; `conda: ../../envs/data.yml`). A `.gitignore` carve-out
`!/data/external/refs/**` commits the assets (git-LFS via `.gitattributes` `*.cm`/`*.fa`/`*.fasta`), while the
rest of `data/external/` stays ignored; `.snakemake/` + `/logs/` added to `.gitignore`. **Outputs.**
`data/external/refs/` — 6 CMs + 5 FASTAs (all git-LFS) + `provenance.json`; `src/tbox_finder/refs.py`;
`workflow/rules/data.smk`; `tests/unit/test_refs_staging.py` (10 guards → 59 unit tests). **Validation.** PASS —
`git lfs ls-files` lists all 11 CMs/FASTAs with LFS oids equal to their pinned sha256 (132-byte pointer blobs);
`provenance.json` is plain git (`filter: unspecified`); `git check-attr filter` == `lfs` on every `*.cm`;
`content_sha256` is LFS-pointer-aware so the checksum test passes in CI (no LFS smudge). `pytest tests/unit`
**59 passed**; `ruff` + `black 26.3.1 --target-version py312` + `ruff format` clean; `snakemake --lint` "good
condition" + `-n` clean. **Decisions.** `data/external/refs/` is the canonical committed home for these immutable
reference assets (§5.2 permits small committed CMs/FASTAs as git-LFS rather than DVC); the `*_master.fa` glob
stages all four MASTER FASTAs (RFGC3V = the combined RF00230/GC/gecont3/Vitreschak set + its three named
components) — omission from an immutable dir is the costly error, and the PRD's descriptive "26,639 seqs" matches
no single file and is not this step's gate. context7 §3.3 (plugin server): git-lfs v1 pointer spec
(`version`/`oid sha256:`/`size`) + `git check-attr filter` / `git lfs ls-files` verification. Consumed by P0-30
(masking/mining CMs), P4 (GATE-1 reference CMs), P6 (validation CMs + blind set). **Commit.** (this commit — the
P0-11a atomic commit on branch `p0-11a-stage-refs`).

## Step P0-12 — 2026-07-09: Data ingest + count/hash parse-correctness gate (23,535 × 107)
**Inputs.** `../tboxdb-master/Master_tboxes.csv` (raw, immutable; 92 MB, `wc -l` = 39,459 — newline-inflated by
embedded newlines in quoted tRNA 2°-structure fields) and `../tboxevo/data/interim/master_clean_v0.parquet`
(the cleaned canonical corpus). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/ingest.py::run_ingest` (driven by the new
`workflow/rules/data.smk::ingest_master`, LOCAL, out of `rule all`, no `input:` — sibling sources external to the
DAG, like `stage_reference_assets`) does a proper RFC-4180 parse (pandas `read_csv`, no `index_col`) → asserts the
**parse gate 23,535 records × 107 raw columns** → reproduces the tboxevo cleaning contract *byte-for-byte* (drop
the leading unnamed pandas-export index column → 106 named cols; coord `0`→NaN sentinel on 15 columns; `discrim_end`
`-2`→NaN; `Regulation` value-set assertion, `Unknown` preserved) → appends a deterministic controlled-SHA-256
per-record `record_sha256` column → computes **per-record hash-identity vs the canonical parquet**. Verified once
that reading-without-`index_col`-then-dropping is value-identical to tboxevo's `read_csv(index_col=0)`
(`df.equals` True), so a single read serves both the 107-col gate and the 106-col clean. **Outputs.**
`data/interim/master_tboxes_ingested.parquet` (106 cols + hash, DVC md5 `7cbecf42…`); `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet`
(106-col training corpus P1–P4 consume, DVC md5 `8356cf24…`); `data/processed/audits/count_parse_report.json`;
`data/interim/master_tboxes_ingested.provenance.json` (six §11 fields; input paths normalized to the rule-relative
sibling refs, content-hashes authoritative); `src/tbox_finder/ingest.py`; `tests/unit/test_ingest.py` (+19: stdlib
hash-contract tier runs in bare CI, pandas tier `importorskip`); `tests/golden/test_ingest_golden.py` + the
100-record `tests/fixtures/ingest_sample/` (+ `expected.sha256`); replaced `tests/golden/test_golden_scaffold.py`.
**Validation.** **PASS** — parse gate 23,535×107 → 23,535×106; **per-record hash-identity 100.0000% (23,535/23,535)**,
our_digest == canonical_digest `f471fdf6…`; golden fixture digest matches; parquet write is byte-deterministic
(stable DVC md5). `pytest tests/unit tests/golden` **79 passed** (data stack) / **68 passed + 11 skipped** in a
bare-pandas CI-test simulation (bare import of `ingest` works; stdlib tier green); `ruff 0.15.15` + `black 25.11.0`
(py311) + `ruff format` clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean. **§20 open item (reported).**
class I:II = **22,334 : 1,200** (+1 unassigned) = **18.61 : 1** from the `type` column (Transcriptional=class I /
Translational=class II; PRD §3, PMID:25583497); per-phylum: **Firmicutes 21,185 = 90.01%** dominant across
**29 phyla** (Actinobacteria 1,230; 453 NaN-phylum) — feeds §12's order-count claim + P0-26 power budget.
**Decisions.** Added **`pyarrow=24.0.0`** to `envs/data.yml` + regenerated `data.conda-lock.yml` (user-approved
env amendment, CLAUDE.md §3.1; additive, no ML/stack-pin change — all existing pins held): pandas+pyarrow is the
*only* path to a genuine 100% reproduction (an all-polars reimplementation diverged at 70.97% on float-format /
null-handling, and tuning it to pass would violate §10.3). The per-record hash is a **controlled SHA-256** over a
canonical row serialisation, not `pandas.util.hash_pandas_object` (pandas-version-dependent), so the hash column +
golden digest are stable and human-auditable. **CodeRabbit (5 findings) — 4 applied, 1 rejected:** the row-hash
encoding hardened to **kind-tagged + length-prefixed** tokens (collision-proof; a real string can't alias the missing
sentinel; `pd.NA` now hashes as missing, not `"<NA>"`); coord coercion made **fail-loud** on non-numeric junk
(§10.3; 0 hits on the corpus → output unchanged); report `passed` tightened to full cleaned shape. **Rejected:**
adding `discrim_end` to the zero-sentinel set — the canonical has **0** `discrim_end==0` records and the tboxevo
contract deliberately excludes it (its sentinel is `-2`), so the change is a no-op that would deviate from the pinned
upstream contract; identity re-verified **100%** after the re-encode (only the interim hash column / digests changed,
processed md5 `8356cf24…` unchanged). **CodeRabbit re-review (2 further findings, both applied):** added an explicit
**column-schema gate** (a value-identical record under a renamed/reordered column can no longer pass —
`columns_match` in the identity report) + **exotic-type hash disambiguation** (a non-str/float/int/bool cell is tagged
`S<type>:<str>` so it can't alias a string) — both robustness-only, real-corpus digests + interim md5 `7cbecf42…`
**unchanged** (TBDB cells are str/float/int/bool). `verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule; DVC push deferred to
phase-exit)`. context7 §3.3: not required — no new third-party library API written (pandas `read_csv`/`to_parquet`
reproduce the pinned tboxevo contract; pyarrow used only as the parquet engine). **Commit.** (this commit — the
P0-12 atomic commit on branch `p0-12-ingest`).

## Step P0-13 — 2026-07-09: Pin the governing GTDB release (+ GTDB-Tk ref-data package check, r220 fallback)
**Inputs.** tboxevo GTDB taxonomy maps (release pin); GTDB `release232/232.0` metadata (`MD5SUM.txt`, `stats/r232`);
GTDB-Tk reference-package index; `src/tbox_finder/provenance.py`. **Process.** A **Q-question stop** (CLAUDE.md §7
item 1) resolved the release: two background investigations established that the tboxevo maps pin **GTDB R232**
(Release 11-RS232, 2026-04-15 — tboxevo ADR-0001, cross-checked: GTDB's `bac120_taxonomy_r232.tsv` MD5 `4d42e137…`
== tboxevo's independent pin) and that R232 has an **available** GTDB-Tk package (`gtdbtk_r232_data.tar.gz`, ≥ 2.7.0),
so PRD §7.2's contingency is satisfied and the **r220 fallback is not invoked**; user approved R232 + ADR-0003 A1
(2026-07-09). `src/tbox_finder/taxonomy.py::pin_release` (stdlib-only, bare-importable) then fetched + MD5-verified the
species-rep crosswalk `sp_clusters_r232.tsv` via `workflow/rules/data.smk::pin_gtdb_release` and ran the species-rep
count gate. **Outputs.** `data/external/gtdb/provenance.json` (committed via a `.gitignore` carve-out; release id +
base URL + GTDB-Tk package/version floor + CC-BY-SA-4.0 license + per-file authoritative MD5s + staged-crosswalk
SHA-256/bytes + published & counted species-rep values); the 49 MB `sp_clusters_r232.tsv` staged but gitignored
(re-fetched by the rule, §5.2); genome→lineage taxonomy + metadata **pinned** (URL+MD5) for on-demand P0-14/15/22/P6
fetch; `src/tbox_finder/taxonomy.py` + `tests/unit/test_release_pin.py` + real `tests/fixtures/gtdb_sp_clusters/`
fixture.
**Validation.** **PASSED** — species-rep count gate: counted `{total 199,923, bacteria 189,801, archaea 10,122,
other 0}` **==** GTDB-published `{199,923 = 189,801 + 10,122}` (fail-loud on drift, §10.3); crosswalk MD5-verified
(`7e4b2fc2…`) + SHA-256 recorded; GTDB-Tk package available → r220 fallback recorded `false`. `pytest tests/unit`
**82 passed / 15 skipped** (bare CI-test sim — taxonomy imports bare, ingest pandas tier `importorskip`); ruff 0.15.15
+ black 25.11.0 + ruff-format clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean (rule out of `rule all`).
**Decisions.** Pinned **R232 over the r220 fallback** because its GTDB-Tk package exists (contingency met) — matches the
sibling tboxevo release (zero §13.2 cross-project crosswalk) and gives the largest, most complete novelty frame
(199,923 vs r220's 113,104 reps; a missing reference lineage is what yields a false "novel" call). Staged **only** the
species-rep crosswalk (row-count == the count gate); the ~120 MB genome→lineage tables are pinned-not-downloaded
(authoritative MD5 from GTDB `MD5SUM.txt`), fetched on demand — keeps the pin step LOCAL-lean. ADR-0003 **Amendment A1**
(user sign-off 2026-07-09) fills the D10 release delegation; no locked decision changed. `verification: local
(deterministic LOCAL rule; no DVC artifact — data/external is immutable/re-fetched, §5.2)`. context7 §3.3: not required
— no third-party library API written (stdlib `urllib`/`hashlib` only). **Commit.** (this commit — the P0-13 atomic
commit on branch `p0-13-pin-gtdb-release`).

## Step P0-14 — 2026-07-09: Union novelty prior reconciliation + NCBI→GTDB projection + unprojectable audit
**Inputs.** TBDB corpus `master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12, 23,535 records; NCBI phylum/class + `TaxId`, no
genome-assembly accession); `RF00230_master.fa` (P0-11a, 6,301 accessions); GTDB **R232** `bac120`+`ar53` taxonomy
TSVs (P0-13, fetched on demand + MD5-verified via `taxonomy.ensure_file`); the curated Vitreschak-2008 literature
occurrences (DOI:10.1261/rna.819308). **Process.** `workflow/rules/data.smk::reconcile_union_prior` →
`src/tbox_finder/priors.py` name-projects every record's NCBI lineage to its GTDB phyla via a curated
over-crediting base map (Firmicutes→`Bacillota*`; Proteobacteria routed by class — δ→Desulfobacterota+Myxococcota,
α/β/γ→Pseudomonadota; CPR→Patescibacteriota; Chloroflexi→Chloroflexota; …), each candidate intersected with the real
R232 phylum universe (fail-loud on a fully-absent mapping); folds in the 622 RF00230-only masking loci (accession-only,
no taxonomy) and the 3 confirmed literature clades; re-derives the no-prior phylum list; runs a fail-loud
no-false-novelty gate (§10.3). Genome-resolution GTDB-Tk placement is deferred to P6 (§13.2) — the corpus carries no
assembly accession, so P0-14 does the §7.2 "finest-available (phylum) resolution" tier. **Outputs.**
`data/processed/priors/union_prior.parquet` (**DVC**, 24,160 records = 23,535 TBDB + 622 RF00230-only + 3 literature;
per-record source/accession/coords/NCBI-display-lineage/`gtdb_phyla_credited`/method) + `union_prior.provenance.json`;
`data/processed/audits/union_prior_report.json` (unprojectable audit + has-prior/no-prior phyla + the projection map +
literature evidence); `src/tbox_finder/priors.py` + `tests/unit/test_priors.py` (15 tests) + `tests/fixtures/priors/`.
**Validation.** **PASSED** — accounting: 23,535 = **23,056 projectable + 479 unprojectable** (2.07% → 453 null-lineage
+ 24 eukaryotic mis-annotation + 2 obscure candidate-division; **map_gaps `{}`**); **23 has-prior / 174 no-prior phyla**
(R232 = 197); no-false-novelty gate green (8 known-T-box GTDB phyla — Bacillota/Actinomycetota/Chloroflexota/
Deinococcota/Dictyoglomota/Desulfobacterota/Pseudomonadota/Synergistota — all has-prior, none on no-prior; **no
`Bacillota*` on no-prior** so Clostridial T-boxes can't be mis-scored novel). `pytest` **96 passed / 16 skipped** (bare
CI sim — priors imports stdlib-only, parquet tier `importorskip`) and **15/15** with pandas; ruff 0.15.15 + black
25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean (rule out of `rule all`).
**Decisions.** **§10.1 evidence gate (Stops)** — an 8-agent literature verification confirmed **3/4** Vitreschak clades
at ≥2 mutually-independent sources (δ-proteo→**Desulfobacterota** [Geobacter/Pelobacter], *not* Myxococcota — matches
the corpus's 38 Deltaproteobacteria rows, all Desulfuromonadales; Deinococcus–Thermus→Deinococcota; Chloroflexi→
Chloroflexota); **Dictyoglomi is single-source** (only Vitreschak 2008; FEBS-2010 + TBDB/Zhu-2021 omit it) → **WITHHELD**
from the curated set + flagged in the audit (**user decision 2026-07-09**, CLAUDE.md §10.1) — Dictyoglomota stays
has-prior via 10 TBDB corpus loci so the no-prior list is unchanged. Over-credit split taxa (safe: over-crediting costs
recall, under-crediting fabricates novelty — §7.2:163). ADR-0006 is **not yet authored** (P0-29); P0-14 builds against
the PRD §7.2/§9.1/§13.3 spec directly (provenance `adr` = "PRD §7.2 (ADR-0006 pending, P0-29)"). Two bugs caught +
fixed pre-commit: R232 CPR phylum is `Patescibacteriota` (not `Patescibacteria`) — stranded 9 known records; and `class`
is a Python keyword so `itertuples`'s attribute access silently nulled every record's class (disabling the Proteobacteria
routing) → switched to positional-tuple iteration. `verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule)`. context7 §3.3: not
required — no third-party library API written (stdlib + the in-repo `taxonomy`/`provenance` helpers; pandas lazy for the
parquet read only). **Commit.** (this commit — the P0-14 atomic commit on branch `p0-14-union-prior`).

## Step P0-15 — 2026-07-09: TaxId lineage re-placement for taxonomy-incomplete positives

**Inputs.** `master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12; NCBI-named lineage cols + per-record NCBI `TaxId`, 0 nulls) +
`master_tboxes_ingested.parquet` (row-aligned `record_sha256` hash-link, since master_clean carries no hash); a
**frozen, MD5-pinned NCBI taxdump snapshot** (`taxdmp_2026-07-01.zip`, NCBI `taxdump_archive`).
**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/taxonomy.py::replace_lineage` (+ `workflow/rules/data.smk::replace_taxid_lineage`, LOCAL,
out of `rule all`, lambda-derived path params) walks each incomplete record's TaxId lineage from the pinned taxdump
(`nodes/names/merged.dmp`, stdlib parse; merged-id redirect + cycle-guard) and **fill-only** recovers only the missing
ranks — the curated 96% is never overwritten. Recovered labels are reconciled to the corpus's **pre-2021 NCBI vintage**
(`Firmicutes`, not modern `Bacillota`) using the **taxdump's own synonym / equivalent-name records** (no hand-built
rename map). **NCBI — not GTDB — is the re-derivation source** (Q-STOP resolved, user 2026-07-09): the TaxId is a native
NCBI id, the split groups on NCBI order strings, and GTDB is genome-keyed so it cannot resolve the environmental /
metagenome / CPR TaxIds that dominate the residue; GTDB R232 stays governing for novelty (P0-14) + P6 placement.
**Outputs.** `data/interim/lineage_replaced.parquet` (23,535 × 16 — `record_sha256`, `TaxId`, `resolved_<rank>` +
`<rank>_source` ∈ {corpus, taxid_recovered, unresolved} for phylum/class/order/family/genus, per-rank
`dropped_from_{phylum,class,order}_holdout`, headline `dropped_from_clade_holdout` = order residue; DVC) +
`data/processed/audits/lineage_replacement_report.json` + `lineage_replaced.provenance.json` +
`data/external/ncbi_taxonomy/provenance.json` (taxdump pin: url/md5/sha256/license/bytes).
**Validation.** **PASSED** — total accounting == **23,535** per rank (present + recovered + still_incomplete;
`accounting_ok`, fail-loud §10.3); recovery per rank **phylum 32/453 (7.06%)**, **class 71/841 (8.44%)**, **order
59/928 (6.36%)** — modest by design, the residue is genuinely-unplaceable environmental/metagenome + MAG-clade-only /
CPR taxa with no formal NCBI rank; **869 `dropped_from_clade_holdout`** (order residue → random-split-only) and the
load-bearing invariant holds: `resolved_order` NULL ⟺ dropped, so **a no-clade record can never silently enter a clade
fold** (PRD §9.2 / §8.2). `pytest tests/unit` **116 passed / 5 skipped** (bare CI sim — pure parse/lineage/reconcile
tier runs; parquet tier `importorskip` pyarrow) and **9/9** with pandas+pyarrow; ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0
(`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n` clean.
**Decisions.** The **frozen governing-release taxonomy** phrase (PRD §9.2) is realized as the **pinned NCBI taxdump**
for re-derivation, not GTDB — a deviation from the P0-13-wired GTDB files, surfaced as a **§7 Q-STOP** (corpus is
NCBI-named at an old vintage + GTDB genome-keyed would corrupt split labels; **user chose Option A** — pinned NCBI
taxdump + vintage-normalization). The 46 genuinely-**new recovered orders** are all Candidatus orders for organisms that
had no order before (not vintage-variants of existing corpus orders → no split incoherence), surfaced in
`new_taxa_introduced` for transparency. `fetch_taxdump` verifies MD5 vs the pin on `download=True` (production), trusts a
pre-staged file on `download=False` (offline/tests). ADR-0004 is **not yet authored** (P0-19); P0-15 builds against PRD
§9.2/§12 directly (provenance `adr` = "PRD §9.2 (ADR-0004 pending, P0-19)"). context7 §3.3: not required — stdlib
(urllib/zipfile/hashlib) + in-repo `provenance` helper; pandas/pyarrow lazy for the parquet join only.
`verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule)`. **Commit.** (this commit — the P0-15 atomic commit on branch
`p0-15-taxid-lineage`).

## Step P0-16 — 2026-07-09: Source the independent non-Firmicutes GATE-1 anchor (arm c)

**Inputs.** The **Vitreschak 2008** supplement (DOI:10.1261/rna.819308, PMID:18359782, PMC2271356) — 3 open-access `.doc`
files (Table S1 abbr→organism crosswalk + Fig S1 T-box-leader alignment + legends), SHA-256-pinned, re-fetched not
committed (copyright); the training corpus `master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12, leakage baseline); the primary NCBI RefSeq
genomes of the confirmed hosts (via E-utilities). **Process.** `src/tbox_finder/anchors.py::source_anchor` (+
`workflow/rules/data.smk::source_gate1_anchor`, LOCAL, out of `rule all`, lambda-derived path param; stdlib-only import)
parses the gap-elided Fig S1 rows in **both** cp1251 + UTF-16LE (`strings -e s`/`-e l`, else the δ-proteo/Deinococcus rows
silently drop) into contiguous genomic segments split by `(N)` omitted-length placeholders, resolves each host assembly →
RefSeq+GenBank replicons (`elink`), and **re-derives each leader from its primary genome by seed-and-extend localizing the
literature segments** (Vitreschak's sequence IS the query — CM-free), requiring the inter-segment gaps to match the elided
lengths as the specificity guard, then extracting the full contiguous leader (elided middle filled from the genome). Each
host is GTDB-placed (name projection to the ≥2-source-confirmed phylum; genome-resolution deferred to P6, matching P0-14)
and leakage-classified vs the corpus (RefSeq+GenBank accession-normalized, min/max-normalized coordinate overlap).
**Outputs.** `data/external/gate1_anchor/` (`.gitignore` carve-out; `.cache/` supplement+genomes re-fetched) —
`gate1_anchor.fasta` (**16** re-derived non-Firmicutes leaders, git-LFS; headers carry accession:coords(strand)|organism|
phylum), `provenance.json`, `gate1_anchor_report.json` (raw counts per clade/host, GTDB placement, independence statement,
leakage report, withheld + unresolved).
**Validation.** **PASSED** — **16 loci** across the 3 confirmed phyla (**Desulfobacterota 2, Deinococcota 5,
Chloroflexota 9**; 8 hosts), **12 exact + 4 approximate** (each with two-segment offset agreement); full provenance;
independence-from-RF00230 documented + the ascertainment-ceiling bound disclosed (reaches RF00230-cutoff-invisible but not
all-consensus-invisible); every host non-Firmicutes at **≥2 independent sources**; leakage report **11/16 corpus-overlap**
(TBDB scanned these hosts via `Vitreschak_master.fa` → held out at P0-24) + **5/16 coordinate-novel** (corrected — see
**Review fix** below) + raw count reported
(the min-N / arm-(c)-fallback verdict is deferred to P0-26). `pytest tests/unit` **122 passed / 23 skipped** (bare CI sim
— anchor: 17 pure-logic + import-purity run, 4 pandas-gated `importorskip`) and **21/21** with pandas+pyarrow; ruff
0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n source_gate1_anchor`
valid.
**Decisions.** **§7 scientific-evidence-gate STOP resolved (user 2026-07-09):** approved **Option A** — independent
organism-genome re-derivation (an 8-agent literature workflow established the supplement is accessible but carries **no
accessions/coordinates**, so loci are re-derived per organism+gene from the primary genome, localized by Vitreschak's own
Fig S1 sequence). **No env amendment** — stdlib `urllib` E-utilities + subprocess `strings` suffice (Biopython, already in
`envs/data.yml`, not needed). **Dictyoglomus WITHHELD** (single-source — only Vitreschak; MMBR-2009/FEBS-2010/TBDB-2021
omit — reproduces the signed-off P0-14 decision); it stays has-prior via TBDB corpus loci but is never a certifying anchor
clade. The existing TBDB-derived `data/external/refs/Vitreschak_master.fa` was **rejected as the anchor** (CM-derived +
corpus-overlapping → exactly the circularity P0-16 exists to prevent). 2 *Dehalococcoides* `ileS` loci withheld as
**indel-divergent** (not gapless-localizable within tolerance; withhold-not-force-fit, §10.3). ADR-0005 not yet authored
(P0-19/25) → builds against PRD §7.1/§9.2(c)/§2.3 directly (`adr` = "PRD §7.1/§9.2(c)/§2.3 (ADR-0005 pending, P0-19/25)").
context7 §3.3: n/a (stdlib urllib/subprocess/hashlib + in-repo `provenance` helper; pandas lazy for the corpus read only).
`verification: local (deterministic LOCAL rule; NCBI E-utilities live-fetch)`.
**Review fix (CodeRabbit, pre-merge).** `localize_leader` returned minus-strand coordinates in **reverse-complement space**
(positions in `revcomp(genome)`), but they flowed unconverted into `classify_leakage` (compared against the corpus's
**forward-genome** coordinates), the audit `start_0based`/`end_0based_excl`, and the FASTA headers — so the 6 minus-strand
loci were leakage-classified in the wrong coordinate frame (§8.2). Fixed to convert **both** strands' coordinates to the
forward genome (`[L−end, L−start)`; the `leader` sequence is unchanged — already sense). This flips **4** minus-strand loci
(**GSU_LEUA, DAC_LEUA, DG_ILES** [corpus stored under GenBank `CP000359`, invisible to a RefSeq-only lookup], **TR_LEUS**)
from coordinate-novel → **corpus-overlap**: each is present in the training corpus and must be held out at P0-24 — a leakage
**miss** the buggy frame had masked. Leakage split corrected **7/16 → 11/16** overlap, **9 → 5** coordinate-novel; FASTA
leader sequences are byte-identical (only headers/coords change). Added a forward-coordinate unit invariant (asymmetric-flank
minus-strand planting, which a symmetric planting hid) + a Tier-2 end-to-end minus-strand-leakage-caught regression.
**Review fix 2 (CodeRabbit re-review, user-approved "apply all 4").** Four localization/reporting hardening findings on the
anchor: **F4** `source_anchor` now **fails loud** if zero loci localize (no silent empty artifacts, §10.3); **F3** replicon
selection is ranked by localization quality (mismatches, then exact offsets — `n_segments_matched` is invariant) and a tie
between **distinct** genomic locations (within a genome or across replicons) is **withheld**, not resolved by iteration
order (§10.3); **F1** the leakage report no longer counts a locus with a same-replicon corpus record that lacks coordinates
as coordinate-novel (`n_novel_no_overlap` → `n_no_confirmed_coord_overlap` + new `n_uncoordinated_same_replicon` /
`n_coordinate_novel`); **F2** the leader boundary is now derived from each segment's **actual matched position** (extended
across the short flanks by nominal spacing) instead of pinning the whole leader to one anchor's nominal offset — so a shifted
(indel) offset no longer mis-sets the boundary. Re-run (offline, cache-only) is **stable at 16 loci / 8 hosts / 12 exact + 4
approximate**, leakage **unchanged at 11/16 overlap** (no locus withheld as ambiguous; F1 `n_uncoordinated = 0` on this
data); **F2 shifts 3 approximate leaders' end coordinate by 1–2 nt** (DR_GLYS2 −1, DEH_TRPB +2, DE_TRPB −2; starts and all
overlap classifications unchanged; `genome[start:end] == leader` invariant re-verified). +5 tests (boundary-tracks-shift,
ambiguous/unambiguous localization, uncoordinated-not-novel, fail-loud-on-empty).
**Commit.** the P0-16 atomic commit on branch `p0-16-gate1-anchor` (`db4c3fc`) + two review-fix commits (squash-merged).

## Step P0-17 — 2026-07-10: additional independent non-Actinobacteria class-II positives + leakage-test
**Inputs.** Published non-Actinobacteria class-II (translational) occurrences (peer-reviewed literature); the 18-record
single-phylum Actinobacteria/ILE natural set (`data/external/refs/heldout_blind_set.fasta`); the training corpus
`data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (read only to enumerate P2 leads).
**Process.** A ≥2-source literature survey (workflow `wf_4bc70b3f-4b5`: 7 discovery angles + adversarial verification over
Consensus/PubMed/Scholar-Gateway/bioRxiv) returned a **verified NEGATIVE**: the peer-reviewed literature documents class-II
(translational) T-boxes **only in Actinobacteria** (the reduced *ileS* system). Every non-Actinobacteria candidate (10 loci
surfaced from TBDB's truncated-Stem-I `type=Translational` flag) was **REFUTED** — TBDB's own curated `Regulation` field is
`Unknown` for all, none has explicit translational-mode evidence, and TBDB annotates a terminator hairpin for them (so the
call is not terminator-absence-based). Per CLAUDE.md §10.2/§10.3, `src/tbox_finder/anchors.py::source_classII`
(`workflow/rules/data.smk::source_classII_positives`, LOCAL, out of `rule all`) publishes an **honest empty positive set**
(raw count 0) + the ≥2-source evidence chain, and catalogues the corpus's **40** CM/DB-derived non-Actinobacteria
`type=Translational` records **by reference** as P2 de-novo discovery **leads** (explicitly NOT positives — ingesting them
would be the CM/DB circularity P0-16/P0-17 exist to break). User sign-off (Option "Withhold + catalogue leads"), 2026-07-10.
**Outputs.** `data/external/classII_positives/{classII_positives.fasta (empty; git-LFS leaves 0-byte files empty),
provenance.json, classII_report.json}`. Report: `raw_positive_count=0`; leads = 40 (Firmicutes 20, Deinococcus-Thermus 11,
`(unresolved)` 4, Chloroflexi 2, Ca. Saccharibacteria 1, Ca. Wolfebacteria 1, Synergistetes 1); highest-spread priority for
P2 = {Chloroflexi, Deinococcus-Thermus}; leakage report **n_positives=0 → nothing added to the P0-24 no-leakage test**.
Evidence chain (≥2 independent, in-repo-cited): Vitreschak 2008 (PMID:18359782) + MMBR 2009 (PMID:19258532) + TBDB 2021
(PMID:32882008) + Sherwood 2015 (PMID:25583497) + M. tuberculosis 2024 (PMID:39097611). New `tests/unit/test_classII_provenance.py`
(2 tiers: 5 bare-stdlib + import-purity, 2 pandas-gated end-to-end incl. the anti-circularity "never emit a lead as positive"
guard).
**Validation.** PASSED — full provenance (6 §11 stamps); positives non-Actinobacteria (vacuous, 0); leakage-tested (0 added);
**raw class-II counts reported** (min-N-reachability verdict deferred to P0-26/ADR-0005). `pytest tests/unit` 125 passed / 24
skipped (bare CI sim; classII 5 pure-logic + import-purity, 2 pandas-gated `importorskip`) + 7/7 with pandas+pyarrow; ruff
0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n source_classII_positives`
resolves. **Decisions.** The absence of a natural non-Actinobacteria class-II positive is itself the scientific gap the project
proposes to close (no database ever ran a class-II model outside Actinobacteria TaxID:1760) — any such element the project
reports must be a NEW de-novo finding (terminator absence + SD sequestration), not a literature citation; the class-II
anti-mimicry pillar (PRD §5 mechanism 3) rests on the 18-record Actinobacteria set + the P2 construction-powered
synthetic-class-II recovery set, as the PRD already anticipates. Withhold-not-fabricate, reproducing the signed-off P0-14
(*Dictyoglomus*) / P0-16 (*Dehalococcoides*) precedents. ADR-0005 (min-N floor) pending (P0-19/25). context7 §3.3: n/a
(stdlib + in-repo `provenance`/pandas-lazy corpus read). **Commit.** the P0-17 atomic commit on branch `p0-17-classII-positives`.

## Step P0-18 — 2026-07-10: Dataset-license-compatibility audit
**Inputs.** Every upstream source feeding the training corpus / curated dataset (TBDB `Master_tboxes.csv`, Rfam RF00230,
tbox-scan `TBDB001.cm`, tboxevo sub-element CMs, 9 PDB crystals, the Vitreschak-2008 supplement, GTDB R232, NCBI RefSeq +
taxdump, the P0-16 anchor, the P0-17 class-II set); ADR-0001 §D9 (release-license gating); PRD header / §2.1 G-E / §7 / §16–17.
**Process.** Verified each source's license against the **authoritative upstream on 2026-07-10** — GitHub About/LICENSE for the
MIT sources (the local `tboxdb-master` / `tbox-scan-master` archives omit the LICENSE, so confirmed against
`github.com/mpiersonsmela/tbox` + `github.com/jamarchand/tbox-scan`); the wwPDB usage-policy for PDB CC0-1.0; EMBL-EBI for Rfam
CC0-1.0; the GTDB downloads page for CC-BY-SA-4.0; NCBI policies for RefSeq/taxdump public-domain — and cross-checked the
source-carried `data/external/**/provenance.json`. Landed `scripts/reconstruct_encumbered.py` (stdlib-only `SOURCES` registry =
single source of truth + checksummed fetch/reconstruct stubs), `docs/license_audit.md` (per-source verdict table + resolution),
`tests/unit/test_license_audit.py`. **Outputs.** 11-source audit; verdicts {9 compatible, 2 encumbered}; the two encumbered
route to reconstruct-from-source — `gtdb` (CC-BY-SA-4.0 share-alike; novelty-prior projection re-applied via
`src/tbox_finder/priors.py`, **not embedded**) + `vitreschak_supplement` (proprietary; anchor leaders re-derived from
public-domain RefSeq via `anchors.py::source_anchor`, supplement never shipped).
**Validation.** PASSED — every upstream source carries a license + compatibility verdict; the release path (own-derived
CC-BY-4.0 + reconstruct script for encumbered sources) is decided + consistent with ADR-0001 §D9. `pytest tests/unit` **157
passed / 24 skipped** (bare CI sim; license-audit 32 pure-stdlib, no pandas gate) — was 125/24; ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0
(`--target-version py312`) clean; no `.smk` touched (DAG lint unaffected). **Decisions.** Q-question RESOLVED — user sign-off
2026-07-10, **Option A**: curated dataset = own-derived fields (coordinates, labels, predictions, splits, calibration,
re-derived anchor, hash-links) under **CC-BY-4.0** + `reconstruct_encumbered.py` for the two encumbered sources (Option B,
embed-everything-under-CC-BY-SA-4.0, declined — share-alike reduces reuse + clashes with the CC-BY-4.0 model weights). GTDB is
the **only** share-alike source, and the corpus lineage-by-rank columns are NCBI-named (P0-15, public-domain taxdump), **not**
GTDB — so the split-assignment table (P0-23) carries no GTDB content; GTDB touches only the union novelty prior, which ships as
own-derived has-prior/no-prior booleans (facts) with the GTDB-name compilation reconstructed. Corrects the P0-11a provenance's
imprecise "see tboxdb LICENSE" (the local archive has none; MIT confirmed upstream). context7 §3.3: n/a (stdlib urllib/hashlib +
in-repo provenance reads). **Commit.** the P0-18 atomic commit on branch `p0-18-license-audit`.

## Step P0-19 — 2026-07-10: Author ADR-0004 (Split & leakage policy)
**Inputs.** Measured all-pairs element-overlap prevalence on `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12; 23,535 records; DVC md5 `8356cf24`, sha256 `1eb76591…b062dd2`) via new `scripts/measure_overlap_prevalence.py`; PRD §8 (precedence), §9.2 (splits/leakage), §2.3+§12 (GATE-4); P0-15 taxonomy-incomplete residue policy; P0-16/P0-17 arm-(c) + class-II sets.
**Process.** Mapped the 7 annotated elements to their `(start,end)` columns, computed all 21 pairs' overlap prevalence + directional containment (inclusive-nt, per-record local frame, `-1`/NaN = absent, min/max-normalised for strand), persisted to `data/processed/audits/overlap_prevalence_report.json`. Authored `docs/decisions/ADR-0004-split-and-leakage-policy.md` (D1–D7): the single-label total precedence order + the measured table; the D2 structure-aware clustering cut; D3 clade-crossing; D4 taxonomy-incomplete; D5 nested single-checkpoint fold; D6 GATE-4; D7 no-leakage CI.
**Outputs.** ADR-0004 (Accepted); `scripts/measure_overlap_prevalence.py`; `data/processed/audits/overlap_prevalence_report.json`. **Three material overlaps** (18 others ≤0.13% → disjoint): **Specifier⊂Stem_I 100.0%** (23122/23122, med 3bp), **Antiterminator∩Terminator 99.54%** (23332/23439, med 11bp), **Discriminator⊂Antiterminator 100.0%** (23208/23208, med 4bp). PRD's "98.3%" reconciled = specifier *presence* rate (23142/23535); conditional containment is total.
**Validation.** PASSED — precedence table complete (all 21 pairs) with measured prevalence; GATE-4 ≥0.80 floor + P0 magnitude rationale + δ-recalibration-by-re-sign-off + blinded-freeze written; D2 clustering distance/coverage cut + sensitivity-sweep + histogram adequacy net pinned; **user sign-off 2026-07-10**.
**Decisions.** New-ADR sign-off (§7 item 2, user 2026-07-10). Two blinded-frozen pinned numbers: D2 clustering cut = **d≤0.30 / identity≥0.70 / coverage≥0.70** (single-linkage, CM-consensus-column distance) — chosen over tighter (0.80/0.80) / looser (0.60/0.70) as the safe over-merge direction without multi-order mega-clustering; D6 GATE-4 = **min per-nt class F1 over {Stem I, Specifier, Antiterminator} ≥ 0.80**. Total precedence order `Discriminator ▸ Specifier ▸ Antiterminator ▸ Terminator ▸ Stem_II ▸ Stem_III ▸ Stem_I ▸ background` resolves every overlap; pseudoknot folded into Stem_II by class definition (not precedence). Clustering-via-CM-alignment is split-only → not a discovery circularity (that lives at §13.3 de-novo MSA, ADR-0006). context7 §3.3: n/a (stdlib argparse/json/hashlib; pandas/numpy lazy for the parquet measure).
**Commit.** the P0-19 atomic commit on branch `p0-19-adr-0004`.

## Step P0-20 — 2026-07-10: Label derivation (8-class per-nt vector + biology precedence + class-II-CM-naive flag)
**Inputs.** Ingested corpus `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12; 23,535 records; DVC md5 `8356cf24`, sha256 `1eb76591…b062dd2`) — per-element coordinates + `type` + aux columns; the ADR-0004 D1 total precedence order + measured all-pairs overlap table; PRD §8 (label derivation), §11 (aux labels), §3 (precedence biology).
**Process.** Mapped each record's 7 annotated element extents onto its per-record local window `[1, tbox_length]` (1-based inclusive; verified `len(FASTA_sequence)==tbox_length` for all 23,535 and every element coord `≤ tbox_length`) via `src/tbox_finder/labels.py::derive_labels`, painting **lowest→highest precedence** so the ADR-0004 D1 order (`Discriminator ▸ Specifier ▸ Antiterminator ▸ Terminator ▸ Stem_II ▸ Stem_III ▸ Stem_I ▸ background`) resolves every overlap; `background` fills the rest. Terminator painted **class-I only** (class II has no terminator; PMID:25583497). Emitted a per-record `label_source`/`class_ii_cm_naive` flag (Translational = `TBDB001.cm`-derived) + a naive label vector that withholds all `TBDB001.cm`-derived structure (class-II record → all background), aux labels (codon/cognate-aa/tRNA-family/regulatory-mode), and element-coverage completeness flags. Rule `workflow/rules/data.smk::derive_labels` (LOCAL, out of `rule all`).
**Outputs.** `data/processed/labels/labels_v0.parquet` (DVC md5 `a6aa61b0`; 23,535 rows × per-record label_string + naive_label_string + flags + aux) + provenance.json; audit `data/processed/audits/labels_report.json`; golden fixture `tests/fixtures/labels_sample/expected.sha256` (digest `2a201ffd…`, derived from the 100-record ingest slice); `tests/unit/test_labels.py` (11 stdlib primitives) + `tests/golden/test_labels_golden.py`. **class I/II/unknown = 22,334/1,200/1** (matches P0-12 §20); **1,200 class-II-CM-naive-withheld**; **413 low-completeness flagged (0 dropped)**; **8 class-I terminator 3′-arm records clamped to the window** (reported, not dropped — no window sequence beyond `tbox_length`). Element-presence counts reconcile with ADR-0004's independently-measured table (Specifier 23,142, Terminator 23,439, Discriminator 23,208, Stem_I 23,475); corpus label-digest `63f39f47…`.
**Validation.** PASSED — every per-nt vector spans its window and is single-label (asserted); precedence applied per ADR-0004 D1; class-II-CM-naive flag present & correct (`class_ii_cm_naive` ⟺ `label_source==class_II_cm` ⟺ Translational; naive vector all-background for those records); low-completeness flagged not dropped (`records_dropped=0`); golden digest matches. `pytest` **171 passed / 27 skipped** (bare CI sim; labels: 11 pure-logic unit + 1 fixture-present guard, 2 pandas-gated `importorskip`) + **13/13** with pandas; ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean; `snakemake --lint` "good condition" + `-n derive_labels` resolves.
**Decisions.** **Scientific-evidence gate (§10.1) CLEARED** — the precedence biology confirmed by ≥2 independent peer-reviewed sources each (no disagreement → verify-and-proceed, and the precedence decision is already signed off in ADR-0004): specifier-in-Stem-I [Grigg & Ke 2013 DOI:10.4161/rna.26996; Wang & Nikonowicz 2011 DOI:10.1016/j.jmb.2011.02.014; TBDB PMID:32882008], antiterminator/terminator mutual-exclusion [Grigg & Ke 2013; Sherwood et al. 2015 PMID:25583497], discriminator-reads-NCCA [Grigg & Ke 2013; PMID:25583497/32882008], class-II-no-terminator [PMID:25583497]. Naive-withholding realized as **all-background for class-II records** (the corpus records no per-element CM provenance → withholding the whole class-II record is the conservative, non-fabricating choice, §10.3; coherent with the anti-mimicry mechanism — a naive checkpoint that still detects class II does so by generalization from class-I structure, not CM memorization; P2 may re-introduce RF00230-derived shared labels). Out-of-window terminator tails **clamped + reported** (not fail-loud) since there is no window sequence to label; an element *starting* beyond the window stays fail-loud. context7 §3.3: n/a (stdlib argparse/json + pandas lazy for the parquet read/write; reuses the ingest hashing contract + provenance writer).
**Commit.** the P0-20 atomic commit on branch `p0-20-label-derivation`.

## Step P0-21 — 2026-07-10: Crystal-structure fixtures (9 PDB) + `tests/unit/test_label_derivation.py`
**Inputs.** The 9 tboxevo T-box structural entries (`tboxevo/data/external/{cocrystal_ss_cons/anchor_<ID>.json, secondarystructure/<ID>/<ID>-2D.dbn}`): 2KZL, 4LCK, 4MGN, 6POM, 6UFG, 6UFH, 6UFM, UCKOPSB, WCBNVLFN; `src/tbox_finder/labels.py` (P0-20, under test); ADR-0004 D1 (precedence) / D6 (9-PDB cross-source ceiling `C`); PRD §8, §7.1.
**Process.** Surveyed tboxevo (18-tool subagent) → **no per-element functional-extent table exists**; only real crystal-derived secondary structure (dot-bracket) + Stem-I `ss_cons`/apical slices (+ a Stem-II slice for 6UFM). Per the **user-signed-off Option B** (2026-07-10), built `tests/fixtures/pdb_element_extents/{pdb_element_extents.json, synthetic_handchecked.json, README.md}`: each of the 9 entries carries only the extents **transcribed verbatim** from the tboxevo files (Stem_I ×5 from `_status=filled` "Stem I domain" / `stem1_length`; Stem_II (30,77) for 6UFM from `stem2_anchor_to_aln_col`; nothing invented — un-resolved elements absent, §10.3), with class from the tboxevo descriptor corroborated by the in-repo ≥2-source literature (ileS ultrashort = class II; classical aaRS = class I). The **exhaustive** ADR-0004 D1 precedence coverage (all 3 material overlaps + residual pairs) lives in the hand-checked synthetic fixture (expected strings hand-authored by simulating the paint order). `tests/unit/test_label_derivation.py` (11 test functions, parametrized) asserts `derive_label_codes` reproduces every fixture vector + the naive-run-withholds-`TBDB001.cm` assertion on the 3 real ileS class-II crystals + an in-frame/consistency tier + a pandas-gated end-to-end `derive_labels` tier.
**Outputs.** `tests/fixtures/pdb_element_extents/` (3 files, 9 crystal entries + 2 synthetic) + `tests/unit/test_label_derivation.py`. **No `labels.py` fixup needed** — the P0-20 derivation reproduces all 9 real crystal extents unchanged (the correct Option-B outcome; the ADR-0004-permitted Specifier-loop widening was declined because the depositions do not resolve a specifier-loop residue extent). **No DVC artifact** (fixtures + test are small git-tracked text). **No `.smk`/`src` change** → DAG lint unaffected.
**Validation.** PASSED — `pytest tests/unit/test_label_derivation.py` **40 passed / 1 skipped** bare (pandas tier `importorskip`) + **41 passed** with pandas; full unit suite **208 passed / 26 skipped** bare (no regression); ruff 0.15.15 + black 25.11.0 (`--target-version py312`) clean. The naive-flag assertion (no `TBDB001.cm`-derived structure in any naive-labeled ileS record; 6UFM production paints Stem_II but naive withholds it) passes.
**Decisions.** **Design/scope STOP resolved (§7 item 1, user 2026-07-10): Option B (file-traceable + hand-checked)** over Option A (full literature §10.1 annotation — a large multi-source task the imp.md CodeRabbit-only budget under-scoped) and Option C (synthetic-only + defer). No new §10.1 gate fired: every extent is a verbatim file fact and class assignment reuses the already-≥2-source-verified in-repo chain (P0-17). **9-PDB cross-source label-noise ceiling `C` (ADR-0004 D6) reported honestly:** only Stem_I ×5 (4LCK/4MGN/2KZL/UCKOPSB/WCBNVLFN) + Stem_II ×1 (6UFM) are residue-resolved — 6POM (full-length) records no functional extent, and none of the 9 resolves Specifier/Antiterminator/Terminator/Discriminator — so a numeric per-nt cross-source F1 ceiling over the full 8-class target is **not estimable** from these depositions (a within-fixture agreement would be circular); one boundary-noise instance observed (WCBNVLFN apical/Stem_I ±pad overhang 1 nt) → `C` shows no sub-floor cap ⇒ **GATE-4 floor stays 0.80** (the N≤9 ceiling must not one-directionally lower the bar). context7 §3.3: n/a (stdlib json/pathlib + pytest; pandas lazy for the end-to-end tier).
**Commit.** the P0-21 atomic commit on branch `p0-21-label-derivation-fixtures`.

## Step P0-22 — 2026-07-10: Split construction (structure-aware CM clustering + nested ladder + clade-crossing rule)
**Inputs.** Corpus `data/processed/master_clean_v0.parquet` (P0-12; `FASTA_sequence` full window + `type`) row-aligned with `data/interim/lineage_replaced.parquet` (P0-15; `record_sha256` + `resolved_{phylum,class,order,genus}` + `dropped_from_clade_holdout`); independent positives `data/external/gate1_anchor/gate1_anchor.fasta` (16, P0-16), `data/external/refs/heldout_blind_set.fasta` (18 Actinobacteria/ILE), `classII_positives.fasta` (0, P0-17); class CMs `RF00230.cm`/`TBDB001.cm` (P0-11a); ADR-0004 D2 (cut)/D3 (clade-crossing)/D5 (nested fold)/D7 (table); PRD §9.2/§12/§5.
**Process.** `src/tbox_finder/splits.py`, 4 rules across 3 envs (data/infernal/viz): (1) `extract-sequences` → per-class window FASTAs + manifest (class I 22,351 = 22,334 Transcriptional + 1 unknown→I + 16 anchor; class II 1,218 = 1,200 Translational + 18 blind); (2) `cmalign` to RF00230/TBDB001 (`--outformat pfam --notrunc --noprob`; afa caps at 10k seqs so Pfam is mandatory for class I; 17.7 min wall, 8 cpu; clen I=224, II=233); (3) `cluster-split` — memory-bounded blocked-GEMM single-linkage union-find over consensus-column identity ≥ 0.70 **AND coverage ≥ 0.70 of the RF00230 model consensus** (ADR-0004 **Amendment A1**, below), whole clusters to one fold, nested ladder (scheme A random genus-stratified 80/10/10 / scheme B leave-one-order-out + class/phylum stress / scheme C independent anchor), the D3 cluster–clade-crossing forced rule + per-scheme diagnostic, the D5 nested most-restrictive train fold (reserve largest orders to ≥60 % coverage → Bacillales+Clostridiales; hold out the other 30 ≥20-positive orders + Actinobacteria phylum + anchor clusters), + the D2 adequacy net (train↔test distance histogram + re-cluster sweep); (4) `plot-figures` (viz env) renders the two PNGs. Vectorized union-find verified identical to scipy `connected_components`; every stochastic choice seeded (`PYTHONHASHSEED=0`, seed 42).
**Outputs.** `data/interim/splits/split_assignments.parquet` (DVC md5 in `.dvc`; 23,569 × 20: `record_sha256`, `cluster_id`, lineage-by-rank, `fold_random`, `loo_order_unit`/`class_holdout_unit`/`phylum_holdout_unit`, `nested_train`/`nested_role`, `is_designated_loo_holdout`/`is_anchor_heldout`/`clade_crossing_cluster`, `dropped_from_clade_holdout`, `parent_record_id`) + provenance.json; audit `data/processed/audits/split_construction_report.json`; figures `figures/split_train_test_distance_histogram.png` + `split_sensitivity_sweep.png` (git-LFS); `conf/data/splits.yaml` (seeded config); `src/tbox_finder/splits.py` + `tests/unit/test_split_assignment.py` (13 tests, 3 tiers). **9,603 clusters, largest 1,238 (5.5 %); roles train 8,303 (35 %) / heldout 8,749 / excluded_clade_crossing 5,652 / dropped 865;** scheme A 18,828/2,354/2,353; clade-crossing order 129 clusters (max 11 orders, 8,453 records) / phylum 51 clusters; 34/34 externals held out.
**Validation.** PASSED (all four gate conditions) — (1) no cluster split across folds (fail-loud `_assert_no_cluster_split`); (2) clade-crossing rule applied + per-scheme count/spread diagnostic reported; (3) sensitivity sweep {0.60,0.70,0.80,0.90} shows the split is **stable-and-conservative under tightening** — nested_train 8,303→13,659→13,863, largest cluster 1,238→139→61, and clade-crossing clusters 129→95→65 for 0.70→0.80→0.90 (held-out order set is cut-independent; tightening yields smaller, purer clusters → fewer clade-crossing exclusions → more training, more separation); only the rejected 0.60 shows mega-cluster residue (largest 17,510).
